text: Bengaluru-based fintech startup PaySwift has raised $12 million in a Series A funding round. The round was led by Sequoia Capital India, with participation from Accel Partners. The company plans to use the funds to expand its payment infrastructure across Southeast Asia. The deal was closed on January 15, 2024.

HealthAI, a Mumbai-based health-tech company, secured ₹150 crore in its Series B round from SoftBank Vision Fund. The investment will help the startup scale its AI-powered diagnostics platform to tier-2 and tier-3 cities in India. Funding was announced on March 3, 2024.
EdTech firm SkillUp raised seed funding of $800,000 from Blume Ventures and Kalaari Capital to expand its upskilling platform for blue-collar workers. The round closed in February 2024.

AgriTech India announced a $45 million Series C round co-led by Tiger Global and Lightspeed Venture Partners. This brings the company's total funding to $78 million. The startup provides AI-driven crop management tools to over 500,000 farmers across India. Deal date: 10th April 2024.

Logistics startup TrackIt has closed a $220 million fundraise in what is being called one of the largest Series D rounds in India's logistics sector. The round was led by SoftBank and included strategic participation from Tata Capital. TrackIt plans to deploy the capital toward autonomous warehouse technology. Announced: 22 May 2024.

GreenLeaf Energy, a Pune-based clean energy startup, raised $30M in a bridge round from existing investor HDFC Capital to support its solar microgrid expansion before its anticipated Series C raise later this year.

Cybersecurity firm DataGuard bagged pre-Series A funding worth $3.5 million led by Matrix Partners India. The startup offers zero-trust security solutions for mid-market enterprises and will use the capital to double its engineering team.

NanoMed, a Hyderabad-based biotech company working on nano-drug delivery systems, raised Rs 80 crore from Nexus Venture Partners in a Series A round. The funds will be used to complete Phase II clinical trials. The investment was made on July 7, 2023.

WealthBot secured $18 million in its latest funding round led by Peak XV Partners (formerly Sequoia Capital India). The AI-driven wealth management startup will use funds to enhance its robo-advisory product and expand into the UAE market. The round was completed on August 30, 2023.

FreightX, an intercity freight aggregation platform, has raised $55 million Series B round co-led by Accel and Tiger Global. The company has processed over 2 million shipments since inception. Funding announced September 2023.

EduSpark today announced the close of its $6 million Pre-Series A round. Investors include Blume Ventures and an undisclosed family office. The company's AI tutoring app has 400,000 monthly active students. The funding will support content localisation into 8 regional languages.

RetailBot has announced raising forty million dollars in Series C from SoftBank Vision Fund and existing investor Lightspeed. The startup's conversational commerce platform is used by 12,000 retailers across India and Southeast Asia.
